in the data file. 

2. The NCPDP "Professional Service Code" (bytes 3 and 4 of this variable) describes what the 
pharmacist did for the patient. This variable is called PROF_SRVC_CD in the data file. 

3. The NCPDP "Result of Service Code" (bytes 5 and 6 of this variable) describes the action the 
pharmacist took in response to a conflict or the result of a pharmacist’s professional service. This 
variable is called RSLT_SRVC_CD in the data file. 

 All six bytes should be populated if any of
